title: dactylorhiza insularis var. bartonii
intro: Dactylorhiza insularis var. bartonii is a rare orchid species native to the Channel Islands, known for its delicate white or pale pink flowers and slender stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits associated with Dactylorhiza insularis var. bartonii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, infusions, or as a tincture, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and optimal usage.
